_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of critical infrastructure systems and software
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of infrastructure-as-code principles and tooling (e.g., Terraform, Pulumi) as applied to data platform provisioning and configuration management.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI DA domain. Knowledge DA-K-011 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-002 (TF, cosine: 0.485), AG-K-006 (AG, cosine: 0.481).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.616). Shared vocabulary: [infrastructure, knowledge]. NICE-only terms [critical, software, systems] vs WIDAI-only terms [applied, code, configuration, data, management, platform] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.878 (P=0.909, R=0.849). P exceeds R by 0.060 — the NICE element's 7 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 18 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of critical infrastructure systems and software" and "Knowledge of infrastructure-as-code principles and tooling (e.g., Terraform, Pul" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.535 < STS 0.616 (gap -0.081). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [critical, software, systems] and [applied, code, configuration] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 18.2%. Shared tokens: [infrastructure, knowledge]. NICE-only: [critical, software, systems]. WIDAI-only: [applied, code, configuration, data, management, platform].
